raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the help focus on substantive argument and metric validity, writing and organization polish, or both?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The section makes high-level contribution and interpretation claims that may need reframing.","R1.2":"The metric definition and later computation description create an argument-chain inconsistency that could invalidate the section.","R1.3":"The empirical conclusions depend directly on whether the reported benchmark setup and results support the claims made.","R1.5":"Because the delegation refers to an entire section, section-level sequencing and narrative arc are central responsibilities.","R1.6":"A broad request for help with a section normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.","RX.1":"Any projected help must disclose uncertainty caused by the broad delegation and limited surrounding paper context.","RX.2":"The section contains strong claims about solved problems and frontier capabilities, making overclaim control load-bearing.","RX.3":"The response must stay within helping with the provided section rather than revising unrelated parts of the paper.","RX.4":"Misstating benchmark implications could downstream mislead readers about agent reliability and ambiguity handling.","RX.5":"Any suggested responsibility must remain traceable to the provided section text and its reported numbers."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the help focus on substantive argument and metric validity, writing and organization polish, or both?

rationale.R1.1: The section makes high-level contribution and interpretation claims that may need reframing.
rationale.R1.2: The metric definition and later computation description create an argument-chain inconsistency that could invalidate the section.
rationale.R1.3: The empirical conclusions depend directly on whether the reported benchmark setup and results support the claims made.
rationale.R1.5: Because the delegation refers to an entire section, section-level sequencing and narrative arc are central responsibilities.
rationale.R1.6: A broad request for help with a section normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projected help must disclose uncertainty caused by the broad delegation and limited surrounding paper context.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains strong claims about solved problems and frontier capabilities, making overclaim control load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The response must stay within helping with the provided section rather than revising unrelated parts of the paper.
rationale.RX.4: Misstating benchmark implications could downstream mislead readers about agent reliability and ambiguity handling.
rationale.RX.5: Any suggested responsibility must remain traceable to the provided section text and its reported numbers.
